description_long: This is a freestanding, full-sized oven with a weathered and heavily used appearance, featuring a rectangular body with a white and gray finish marred by dirt, rust, and scratches. The front panel is equipped with five round control knobs, a horizontal handle, and a large oven door with a darkened window, all accented by orange-brown highlights. The stovetop has four gas burners arranged in a grid, each with visible grates and signs of wear. The sides of the oven are plain and stained, while the back panel displays utility connections, a power cord, and inspection stickers. The oven's overall design is utilitarian, with robust metal construction and minimal decorative elements. Its distressed look suggests it has been exposed to harsh urban environments or heavy use.
description: A large, freestanding oven with a stained white and gray finish, four gas burners on top, and a front panel featuring five knobs, a handle, and a windowed oven door. The appliance shows significant wear and rust, giving it a rugged, urban appearance.
description_view_0: The left side of the oven, showing a plain, stained, and weathered white metal surface with no visible features or controls.
description_view_1: The back of the oven, featuring utility connections, a power cord, inspection stickers, and a gray metal panel with visible rust and grime.
description_view_2: The right side of the oven, similar to the left, with a plain, dirty white metal surface and no controls or openings.
description_view_3: The front of the oven, displaying five round control knobs, a horizontal handle, a large oven door with a dark window, and orange-brown accents, all set against a stained white and gray background.
attribute_control_panel_design: The control panel is located above the oven door and features five evenly spaced, round knobs with an orange-brown color. Above the knobs are two thin horizontal lines, possibly for labeling or decorative purposes. There are no visible digital displays or touchscreens, and the controls appear purely mechanical. The panel is simple, with minimal labeling and no illuminated indicators.
attribute_door_handle_type: The oven door handle is a straight, horizontally mounted bar with a rectangular cross-section, finished in an orange-brown color. It protrudes outward for easy gripping and appears to be made of metal or coated metal, with no visible ergonomic padding or decorative flourishes.
attribute_window_presence: present; a rectangular, dark-tinted window is centered in the oven door, allowing a view into the oven's interior.
attribute_ventilation_features: There are several small vent holes and grilles visible on the lower portion of the back panel, likely for heat dissipation and airflow.
